rows.item: 学習意欲・授業参加度

summary:
The 2030 Agenda for Sustainable Development, adopted by all United Nations Member States in 2015, provides a shared blueprint for peace and prosperity for people and the planet, now and into the future. At its heart are the 17 Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), which are an urgent call for action by all countries - developed and developing - in a global partnership. They recognize that ending poverty and other deprivations must go hand-in-hand with strategies that improve health and education, reduce inequality, and spur economic growth – all while tackling climate change and working to preserve our oceans and forests.
Education for Sustainable Development is an integral part of Quality Education, the United Nation’s Sustainable Development Goal number 4 (SDG4). It is also recognised as a key enabler of all the other SDGs, making sure that learners have the knowledge, skills, values and attitudes they need to understand and tackle the global challenges of today and tomorrow. These challenges include climate change, inequality, global citizenship and the appreciation of cultural diversity.
aims: The objectives of this course are for students to learn about the United Nations Special Development Goals and by so doing develop as global citizens.
goals: Students will learn about the 17 United Nations Sustainable Development Goals
Students will learn how they can promote and implement the 17 United Nations Sustainable Development Goals on local and global levels
Students will use their critical thinking skills to find solutions to global issues
Students will develop English as a global language skills that will enable them to interact and cooperate as global citizens

plan.text:
Global Attitudes and Action
Students encounter their complicity in negative global outcomes. This can include, as an extension of the self, national complicity too.

textbooks: Materials for this course are available through MOODLE. In keeping with the theme we will try to avoid using paper!
prereq: This course is taught in English and the supplied learning materials are in English. Students who wish to take this course should be able to understand spoken and written English at EIKEN 2 level.
prep: Participants in this course are required to complete the both in-class online assignments, as well as complete any homework assignments.

extra.label: 成績評価に関する補足
extra.text:
This course uses a formative system of evaluation. Participants are expected to complete the assigned in-class online activities as well as the final report and presentation.
このコースでは形成的な評価システムを使用します。参加者は、授業内で割り当てられたオンラインアクティビティ、最終レポート、およびプレゼンテーションを完了することが求められます。
